channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 95 replies — 380.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 95 • Updated: 2026-06-13 22:52 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355301
>>62342770
I haven’t worked in 1,5 months and i can already feel myself wanting to just become passive and not really being impressed or motivated by anything (brain getting fried by dopamine and serotonine). You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.

62355307
>>62355301
You’re pathetic. Since a little kid, all the classmates of my have been retired.

62355313
>>62355301
> You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
You’re probably just poor. People making a top 10% income from investments tend not to work.

62355322
>>62355301
>1.5 months
You don’t even have a non working lifestyle built yet.

62355326
>>62355307
This may be the funniest claim I've seen you make. Absolutely nobody from your school does any kind of work at all? Most people do find things to keep themselves occupied with, beyond mere shitposting on an anonymous uzbekistani finger painting notification board.

62355338
>>62355326
Maybe we grew up in different classes.
On one hand, /biz/ loves to larp as rich and successful and on the other hand, loves to larp as muh productive and I don’t like doing nothing.
Fucking jokes.
I hear people say what you say all the time. Yet look at retired people, I don’t see you saying shit to them. Yet when it is someone young and not working, suddenly they are “unproductive.” Even though these people are clearly rich.

62355342
>>62355307
>>62355313
>>62355322
Kek did i trigger you too much?

62355360
>>62355342
I have never in my life looked at someone not working and thought anything bad.
Growing up, my dad retired when I was like 5. By 10, the parents of all my school friends were retired long ago.
By past college, the differences between the haves and have nots became noticeable because college is where you truly see class differences because high school and under tend to mainly kids from your neighborhood.
You cannot imagine life without work yet kids that grew up like me were surrounded by adults that don’t work. It is the normal. People like me just carry on the lifestyle.
People from successful families don’t break their back.

62355375
>>62355301
Depends what you mean by work. But any occupation today is not a good use of time. Like work as in what? Something you need to get a college degree for? White collar?
Or something blue collar? That’s even dumber.
Starting your own business? Okay sure, if you want to start it as a hobby..?
I don’t know. People build lifestyles when they don’t need to work to live.
Some travel, some have many social gatherings
>Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
Oh yeah, the bottom 90% definitely feel alive struggling lol.
Struggling for what? Lmao.

62355404
>>62355301
Sometimes I see the kids in my neighborhood. This isnt an ultra rich 100m a year neighborhood or millions a month neighborhood but in 10-20 years when these kids grow up, it would be a waste of time for them to get a “job.”
Get it?

62357658
>>62355301
Rabbis rabbiposting as usual. Nice try but no, i won't work for (((you)))

62359024
>>62342770
I have 300k...where should i buy a house and get on gibbs? Caliwali?

62359462
>>62342700
I mean someone has to like do stuff u need someone to actually do stuff

62359491
>>62342770
This is called capitalism btw. You either exploit others or u work until you die. Hello knock knock HELLO this is basic capitalism. Wake up everything u guys complain about is due to market forces the Jews are a fantasy of cohesion to keep your little world simple bigga

62360989
Never worked a day in my life and I would never shit on a trade they keep my water running and the electricity flowing. Would you trust a 3rd worlder to wire up your outlet in your house and not expect to die in your sleep when that shit catches fire fuck no I'm going to pay a white American 150$ an hour to do that shit right. Without their goods and services our money doesn't mean shit and I might aswell get raped by a pack of niggers.

62361159
All work is automateable. No one of you should feel safe.

62361494
>>62344611
>the producing members
The secret is these types wouldnt know what to do in life other than work. Their life centers around it. You're supposed to exploit this in them

62363064
>>62361159
What about work on new products that have never been fully before
Someone has to build it first for a robot to even try

62363066
>>62360989
Based

62363074
>>62342905
BMW stock is worth about $25 kek

62363190
>>62361494
Yeah.
Half of Americans own less than 200k in networth but these people try to call me a loser cause I don’t need work.

62363514
>>62342700
The funniest part of this larp spam where you autistically have back and forth conversations about class and wealth with normies is that even in your fantasies you hold a captive audience of blue collar workers and poor office wagies, meaning that whatever your position in life, you're really not that far above them.

62363942
>>62363514
Shut the fuck up loser.
I am way above them.
They are called the average. Irl, most people are office workers at best.
Then you have business owners (this doesn’t automatically mean they’re rich) and you have those in higher tier salaries.
Online, there are more ruralfags and suburban people and/or blue collar.
That’s literally what it is like.

62363944
>>62363514
Watch your fucking mouth.

62363954
>>62363514
I am so far above them mon frere.
I live in a big city and there are tons of rich people here so I was uh huh, average salary here definitely 150k to 200k.
Nope, it is $65k.
Do you know how fucking poor everyone is you pathetic worm scum.

62364016
>>62342700
>Why are blue- collar workers like this?
Because they are stupid
>Are they aware of how the world perceives them?
I don't think they have thoughts like a real human. They simply toil. If you can't be white collar you should just be Neet.

62364222
>>62360989
I work in commercial hvac and I can tell you whites are as prone to fucking shit up as anyone else. Had to go behind one coworker who miswired a brand new 20hp phase motor for a retail store and caused it to burn up. Left the main floor without cooling for 2 days in the middle of summer. Another building I’m working at had a new vrf system installed prior to us getting the maintenance contract. The original install was 2 years ago and they are still out there chasing their fuck ups. All white people.

It’s like people who are loyal to a specific brand because muh made in usa. They would rather pay a premium for the same shit quality product that’s as bad, or often times worse, than Chinese stuff. Just look at the shit Project Farm tests out on his channel to get an idea of what I’m talking about

62364226
>>62364222
Nothing Made in Usa is quality. Lol.
All the top tech factories are in China. If a product is shit but made in China, that’s because the product was meant to be shit.

62364227
>>62364222
I work in a building serviced by commercial HVAC and get to sit in a chair all day. Much better.

62364396
>>62364227
All the “building engineers” (ie. facilities guys) I deal with have to put up with micromanaging along with all the retarded shit that people in the building complain about. I couldn’t do it. I’d rather drive around in my company paid vehicle, taking breaks whenever I want, schedule my own work, work alone, and shitpost on my company paid work phone while making bank.

item.title: >DUDE JUST BUY A HOUSE LMAO what if your corrupt local government approves several AI data centers and they get built 10… — 49 replies — 196.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>DUDE JUST BUY A HOUSE LMAO what if your corrupt local government approves several AI data centers and they get built 10…

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-06-13 22:53 UTC

OP

>DUDE JUST BUY A HOUSE LMAO
what if your corrupt local government approves several AI data centers and they get built 100 feet away from your house? why would i want to risk this?
>THAT WILL NEVER HAPPEN
it's happening all over the US.

Early replies (first 3)

62358792
>don't buy a house!!
>what if someone builds a wearhouse and puts gpus inside of it!!!
really retard?

62358798
>>62358792
the "what if" could be extended to any number of scenarios.
>halfway house gets built next door
>neighbor turns his SFH into a section ape rental

62358803
>>62358792
>>62358798
also the word is "warehouse" you 60 IQ simian niggerbeast.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359791
>>62359499
Kys chang you will never be leader in ai

62359886
>>62359755
i lived in northeastern PA during the marcellus shale "boom." most of the jobs went to out of state workers and, 15 years later, all that's left of the "boom" in my hometown is poisoned wells and a few 3 story hotels that are owned by indians. if you still believe in the industrial job creation meme in the year 1980+46 you are literally retarded.

62360158
>>62359886
My PA mountain house land gets me 100k a year through fracking and I only go there to hunt a couple times a year. Thanks for playing sweety

62360489
>>62360158
limp wristed LARP tell. fracking is a process, not a metric on which royalties are paid. i know this because my parents are paid monthly royalties on a well that sits on their property. they also decrease hyperbolically (the well is re-worked) and so most people (including my parents) are getting basically nothing these days. last time they mentioned it to me, it was something like $100 a month.

62360795
>>62358781
>Those massive circles in Utah
>The cluster in Arizona
Where's all the water going to come from?

62360996
>>62360489
>they mentioned it to me, it was something like $100 a month.
You're parents sound like retards kek. Washington County stays winning. Until I take all it's resources lmao

62361000
>>62360795
Uh buddy I don't know how to tell you this but water comes from the wall. Or a hose if your wall has a hose.

62361003
>>62360795
>Where's all the water going to come from?

how much water do server and electronics need

62361006
>>62358781
IPO was a success. nothing you can do but seethe, lol.

62361042
>>62358781
surprised they wouldn't use the loopholes to take your house too. you don't really own anything if you aren't close to the people in power

62361046
>>62359755
Datacenters create incredibly few jobs actually. They increase work during construction, then hire an H1B skeleton crew.

62362112
>>62360795
They need to ban evaporation cooling for data centers. Let them do the extra steps to either radiate the heat into the air or run their hot water back into the grid.
>>62361003
Most of these data centers use evaporation towers to get rid of the massive amounts of heat those GPUs generate. This is the cheapest way to cool those data centers, especially when corrupt local government provides you with cheap fresh water.

62362134
Why doesn’t the orange retard just take Greenland and put the data centers in the ice

62362146
>>62358781
>tfw used to live in NoVa 7 years ago
I used to live 5 miles away from a chilled beverage distribution center and the monotonous everpresent EEEEEEEEEEEEEEEENNNNHHHH their chillers made pissed me off to no end for 3 years straight, day and night, no holidays off. If I had to deal with 10 of those at once within the vicinity of my home things would get very very violent very very quickly.

62362171
>>62358781
How long until data center construction constitutes eminent domain?

62362554
>>62358781
I’m surprised the west coast has such a small amount of centers. Hippies aren’t so bad after all
Texas looks fucked. Thank god they don’t have all those rules and regulations of California
Kek

62362585
Idaho it is

62362850
>>62362112
data centers use globally in one year the same amount of water the US uses for agriculture in a single day
>they need to ablooablooo
they need to stop listening to retards
>>62362554
california is one the most water strained states in the US and they waste it on planting almonds for said hippies.

62364056
>>62362171
>he doesn't know
https://www.11alive.com/article/news/local/coweta-homeowner-claims-georgia-power-is-undervaluing-home-in-an-eminent-domain-dispute/85-b6e3d335-3f99-4e12-badf-e46961de31b9
https://ohiocapitaljournal.com/2026/06/10/ohio-farmers-fear-new-proposal-would-allow-data-centers-to-take-property/

62364063
Homeowners do everything they possibly can to fuck over people that struggle with housing

So good. I hope the datacentres take their jobs as well. Build more datacentres, a thousand times more of them.

62364064
>>62358803
What about a warhouse? That would be even worse.

62364066
>>62364064
>500,000 square foot whorehouse being built next door

62364067
>>62358781
i know an old guy who owns this house right outside shithole apartments. they were nice apartments once, and the neighborhood was good. he put metal shutters on his doors and windows. his house is like a fortress.
you adapt, or you sell. still better than renting.

62364096
>>62364066
Imagine living by a horrorhouse around Halloween. Very hectic.

62364252
>>62358781
oh no the warehouse full of gpus!

62364256
>>62358781
Based florida (shithole of Miami doesnt count)

62364346
>>62358781
Just live in a good area lmao stop being so poor

I live on a lake in a gated community we aren’t gonna get any data centers here, I hope the state allows more to be built around me, it’s good for my business

62364393
>>62358781
that they're paying a premium to rezone and build in expensive highly populated areas with strained utilities instead of just out in the boonies is more proof that AI is intended as a torture machine for goys

62364398
>>62358781
Don’t buy a house in a poor area.

62364403
>>62364393
The only thing that gives me joy anymore is poorfag suffering. Have I become insane?

item.title: Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. — 38 replies — 152.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude.

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-06-13 22:55 UTC

OP

Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.

https://worldofclaudecraft.com/

Early replies (first 3)

62362742
oldschool runescape is free

62362747
looks like shit
>If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.
sure if you can scam some subhumans but you're competing with 1 billion jeet scammers

62362749
>>62362737
Buy
An
Ad
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362866
>>62362843
>>62362844
kek

62362946
>finally get it to work
>say nigger in the chat
>get banned

kek

62363056
AI reminds me of peak crypto mania yet somehow even more cringe

62363065
>>62362806
Pick your turnips and shut the fuck up.

62363118
>>62362946
works for me

62363279
Janky but cute. Call me in 10 years

62363329
runescape was absolute horseshit 20+ years ago. Lord knows why people played that shit when games like WoW were out.

62363336
>>62363329
Free vs paid subscription

62363356
>>62362737
Thig garbage harvests your IP, geolocation and gives you tracker cookies.
Yeah no thanks jeet OP. This probably has a keylogger too.

62363392
>>62363336
Never could imagine how poor you'd have to be for the cost of WoW to be a deterrent.

62363399
>>62363356
why is everything pozzed now?

62363619
>>62363356
Mother FUCK
I played it for like 10 mins. WTF do I do now

62363621
>>62362753
Kek

62363623
>>62362753
If thats a screenshot, there isnt a single thing goog ole days about it. Thats as globohomo of a game ive ever seen.

62363625
>>62363623
Where is the homo?

62363626
>>62363619
Check your metamask, thanks for all your tokens anon

62363634
None of this shit i even necessary. Mangos effectively opensourced every version of wow that doesnt suck already.

62363642
>>62363634
I don't think thats the point

62363653
>>62362737
>>62362737
>If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.
How much money did you make anon? Post gains.

62363987
>>62363619
I played it for 9 hours, reached max lvl and completed all quests. WTF do I do now?

62364002
>>62362742
So the Jannies can afford it. Very kind of them.

62364043
>>62363623
Yeah but being able to spam "nigger" is pretty old school, most modern games would fine a way to permaban you for that

62364090
>>62363642
it is the point, these retarded scriptkiddies think it's amazing to pay anthropic to shit out something worse then something already available for free. all claude can do is copy open source code

62364293
>>62364090
Claude can do so much more than you think. get good at prompting

62364348
>>62364090
When are you retards going to learn? LLMs learn in almost the same way humans do. They understand things conceptually.

62364357
People with balls played this MMO

62364390
>>62363118
it's not automatic, there's literally a mod that goes through the chat logs and bans anyone who says nigger

62364391
>>62362753
So you get to be a funko pop?

62364400
sounds gay

62364408
>>62364390
What's he get paid?

item.title: So, SPCX. Worth adding to my BOTZ/JEDI stack? — 18 replies — 62.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
So, SPCX. Worth adding to my BOTZ/JEDI stack?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-13 22:39 UTC

OP

So, SPCX. Worth adding to my BOTZ/JEDI stack?

Early replies (first 3)

62364003
>I'm a fucking failure and I envy someone that's better than me
Ok? Weird thing to post on social media but you do you I guess

62364012
>infertile hag with a pro abortion profile picture fudding SPCX
bullish

62364017
Imagine working for 40 years across the 4 biggest bullruns ever and not even managing to get a penny to your name. Normies deserve it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364022
>>62363943
I hate Elon Musk because he is African.

62364031
>>62363943
>I spent the last 37 years of my working life consooming instead of saving my money
>this is why we need socialism
I fucking hate poor people. They're all a bunch of stupid, lazy, greedy faggots.

62364132
My father always told me that if you reach your 40s without a property you have done some something wrong with your life.

62364142
>>62364132
What if you get a property and your ex takes it away from you?

62364173
>>62364142
The secret is to rent out your property and live in another rented place. Crisis avoided.

62364228
>>62364017
This. Fucking loser had two REALLY GOOD opportunities to slurp up a cheap house, with dirt cheap interest rates sustained for a decade. He will get zero fucking sympathy from me. Hope he works door dash until he's 90 years old. This is the kind of person who says "why save, you may die tomorrow!"

62364254
>>62364228
>>62364132
t.
house was paid for by your parents

62364267
>>62364254
Nah. I am a "financial mutant" that can live happily on an extremely small amount and I invested/saved like 60% of my income for like 7 years to get plenty to put down on a house. I did the house hacking thing where I have 3 roommates pay my mortgage for me, that way I can put like 80% of my income into retirement now.

I will admit my mom gave me her old car. It's ugly as sin, but super reliable, so there's that I guess. If I grew up in the boomer economy or started saving money before 2008 house crash, I'd be retired at 40 most likely.

62364275
>>62364267
if you spent 7 years of blood sweat and tears for a down payment, and then more years of the same working down the debt
how can you not have any sympathy for someone under 40 that doesn't outright own a house?

it's not easy to pay off a mortgage before you're 40.

62364291
>>62364275
I try to have sympathy and rationalize "not everyone is as financially responisble as me" but some of these fucking losers still have no excuse.

There's a huge difference between 40 and 52. I would never judge someone who didn't have a house paid off by 40, if it's a 30 year mortgage then theyd have to start paying at 10. Even at 52, I could easily understand not having the mortgage fully paid off, but for a 52 year old to say they aren't even making headway on equity? It's either someone who spent their whole life making bad decissions, or someone so unmotivated they thought they could coast through on life working at mcdonalds.

62364306
>>62364291
I don't know many 50 year olds. I'm in my early 30s. Everybody in my generation is either underwater on a mortgage, renting, or their house was paid for by their parents.

62364314
>>62364306
I highly recommend trying to budgetmaxx like I did. I guess I'm lucky because I can be happy with very little and most expensive things that people desire I don't care much about.

But if you live this lifestyle for even 6 months, you can save some money to put towards debt, and maybe you'll realize you actually don't need those subscriptions or unlimited cell phone data to be happy.

Most young people should probably get roommates. Most young people nowadays I talk to act like it is their divine right to have their own living space, and having a roommate is third world behavior when it can literally cut your living expenses in half. Just do it for like 5 years to get the home downpayment at least.

If you do all of this, and you still can't make financial headway, then you simply MUST get a better job. I spent like 12 months where I almost had no days off because I spent 5 days a week at school/clinicals and 2 days a week working, but now I have a pretty good paying job. If you have a shit paying job and don't do anything to improve it, you're going to wish you sucked up the 1 difficult year instead of 60 years of never having a break from the minimum wage job

62364358
Can't wait for quadrillionaires and zillionaires

62364362
So much seethe over it haha

You can tell it’s a mad white woman over the profile pic too

62364370
>>62363943
Holy Christ can any of you answer the fucking question without sperging out about the fucking screncap?

item.title: He's all about 'Space X' now but what about Dogecoin, which was his first love. — 9 replies — 29.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
He's all about 'Space X' now but what about Dogecoin, which was his first love.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-13 22:39 UTC

OP

He's all about 'Space X' now but what about Dogecoin, which was his first love. Will Musk champion Dogecoin or be a hypocrite, we are still waiting for a $4.20 DOGE

Early replies (first 3)

62363072
>>62363024
>we are still waiting
it's just you and like 6 other guys
everybody with triple digit IQ sold that piece of shit years ago

62363078
>>62363072
A man cannot state his love for one coin then shill another...

62363079
>>62363024
The face of a man who will find himself sentenced to life in prison for defrauding the American people. I hope they actually go to jail when it finally comes crashing down though. None of that SBF shit where only he goes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62363281
>>62363024
Blackest gorillanigger south african kek

62363334
>>62363079
Future generations will be left wondering how he got away with it for so long

62363344
>>62363334
can you imagine being his kids and inheriting a 1T+ dollar fortune

god damn nigger i mean there is generational wealth and then whatever the fuck this is.

62363417
>>62363344
Is this the reason is makes so much kids? That not one can inherit and waste it all?

62363665
>>62363024
DOGE is going to $10 per coin WAGMI
>>62363072
cope tranny

62364368
>>62363344
his kids are trannies

>>62363417
all his kids are trannies

item.title: What's wrong with just DCA'ing BTC, ETH, and SOL and not worrying about what its doing? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's wrong with just DCA'ing BTC, ETH, and SOL and not worrying about what its doing?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-13 22:44 UTC

OP

What's wrong with just DCA'ing BTC, ETH, and SOL and not worrying about what its doing?

Early replies (first 3)

62362176
Nothing wrong with it, but you should be using the ETH or SOL to bet on a memecoin thats <100k mc that you think has a chance of going to 100M-1B mc

62362880
>>62362160
eth, sol, ada, link, all the shitcoins are going to zero.
you should only hold btc, perhaps some monero if you feel frisky.

62362941
>>62362160
This is essentially what I did between 2018 and 2024 (bought BTC, ETH, and little bit of LINK/AAVE/ADA). I made it and no longer go to work.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362966
>>62362160
Why didn't the alligator fight back?

62363027
>>62362966
It's a caiman and a small one at that

62364298
>>62362176
Like what?
>>62362880
I don't believe SOL is going to zero. I would love to own a lot of xmr but my current hardware wallet doesn't support it.
>>62362966
The caiman's spinal cord was crushed by the jaguar (experienced ones know how to kill them quickly)

62364377
Because crypto is unlikely to 10x ever, whereas the S&P does so every 40 years.

item.title: It's not my fault for losing money. The market is behaving irrationally and punishing smart investors AKA the bearish. I… — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
It's not my fault for losing money. The market is behaving irrationally and punishing smart investors AKA the bearish. I…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-13 22:46 UTC

OP

It's not my fault for losing money.
The market is behaving irrationally and punishing smart investors AKA the bearish.
I take zero responsibility for any monetary losses. It's this irrational market's fault.

Early replies (first 3)

62364166
Eat my inflation Bobo! Don't sell everything at once if you don't like it.

62364186
>>62364124
Whatever keeps you warm at night, loser.

62364276
>>62364124
this is why i'm a sports gambler
you make a bet and there's a result

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364367
It's just a predictable weekend pump and dump. It was way oversold short term and obviously going to do a low volume relief pump this weekend.

62364369
Bobo, it's only because exchanges and whales can see the order books and have algorithms to intentionally counter trade against whatever organic retail decides to do (buying spot or shorting).

Yes, it's highway robbery but crypto is unregulated. What are any of us going to do about it?

62364383
>>62364369
That's the old news. It turns out the whole purpose for crapto was to train bots to control the market before tokenizing traditional assets and having scam bots take over those markets as well.
GME was an early test run for this. You make have noticed how its market behavior was similar to crypto.
It has only just begun.
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/03/ai-agents-morgan-stanley-wealth-management-funnel.html
Unfortunately, one thing they really can't get around is limited liquidity. Which is exactly why they need bots to more efficiently extract liquidity from you.

62364376

62360577

62360584

>Link down another -30% since
Sorry bro you better get used to the flapjack feets

62360663
>>62354976
did you take the vax?

62360802
>>62360066
What if link is actually worth something but you can't use it to buy bread. Like kinda like if you had an entire continent in your name but you still need the knowledge to extract resources, work with local governing bodies etc etc

Basically what if we have tokens but are unable to use them because we're goyim

62360825
>>62360137
>duuuuuude it's all a consssssssthhhhpirathy!!!

there he is everyone, our very own sleuth

excuse me, DETECTIVE sleuth

nothing, and i mean NOTHING, gets by this guy. he can sniff out colluding posts in seconds. this guy is GOOD, and i feel ashamed at ever trying to get one by him

afterall, everyone knows that there's nothing more important than guarding the reputation of a dead altcoin on a dead forum


well, you know what we say here...

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62360858
>>62360802
Well, then we're fucked either way.

62360859
>>62360825
>>62360405
Jesus loves you

62360865
>>62360859
religion is the opiate of the masses
or in this case, the stink marines

62360873
>>62360865
That doesn’t change that fact that I don’t hate you and I forgive you.

62360876
>>62360873
your "forgiveness" is rejected, stinkie
how does that make you feel

62361417
>>62360876
feels to me like you're gonna take my whole cock in your ass, and you're gonna like it.

62361431
>>62354976
2030

This year was always the plan for chainlink

62361469
>>62360876
Lilla My, why do you have so much hatred for stinkies?

62361506
I thought green man sergay was going to make me rich...

62361627
>>62358895

62361699
>>62354943
7 years
7 dollars

62361781
>>62361773

62362456
it's very easy, either it works or I'll keep my stack as a lesson in humility

there is no scenario where I sell

62362461
>>62362456
cool beta attitude, stinker
just admit you lost the game of el lifio

62363300
>>62359501
>>62360137
>>62360331
Jesus is the way truth and life but link is equivalent to the money changer parasites he flipped the tables on who ultimately lose. Invest accordingly.

62363331
>>62363300
truth
jesus would rebuke all linkies
"i do not know you, stinkie"

62363473
>>62361781
CL twitter has been spamming about this shit for years now, nothing is hidden, yet the token price is still garbage

62363725
No matter who wins, the butthurt will be everlasting.
The entertainment is guaranteed

62363750
>>62363473
its effectively hidden from retail because the only thing retail understands is blatant advertising and price. link doesn't get shilled to normalfags the way shit like xrp does (ive never met a single person irl who has heard of chainlink), and nobody cares because the price has been stagnant for 5 years. its actually the perfect setup for a takeoff, though obv even me saying that will make you respond 'bearish'. cold hard truth is this is what disbelief looks like, you are demoralized and nothing will change that but radical price action.

62363975

62364260

62364322

still 300 m tokens left to dump
SWIFT blockchain is a meme
The reserve is a meme

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 71 replies — 121.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-06-13 22:22 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362689
>>62361452
Of all the famous billionaires, Musk's companies are the only truly innovative ones. Like many of us, I don't like his liberalism or his transhumanism, but it's undeniable that the "hype" that fuels his investments is based on the significant chance that he can pull of what he says. Afterall everyone wrote off starlink and autonomous cars.

I do find it irritating how people can't talk about him or spacex in good faith. They hide behind investment language they don't understand when the truth is that they hate him and his companies because the media told them to. The regime media has had it in for him ever since he bought twitter since they had a near total monopoly on information before that point. You're seeing just how much they hate the situation by the fact that the UK grooming gang discussion in parliament was hijacked by deranged leftists into a 2 minute hate against Musk.
These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example, a truly hideous "person" who has done nothing except harm humanity and steal money.

62362700
>>62361452
Now that he's a trilly, do you goys think this bitch will still settle for that $500k/year he was giving her or will that 10x soon?

62362803
>>62362689
>they hate him and his companies because the media told them to
I simply hate all billionaires because it's fucked

62362809
>>62362689
>The regime media
the ones who pushed EVs?
>These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example
you mean the currency manipulator who "broke the bank of england" ?
who did he steal money from?

62362814
>>62361452
In a sea of grifting the guy offers at least a unique perspective on his multiple scams. For that he is awarded 1 trillion dollars

62362818
>>62361452
ask yourself which space sector company can dislodge SpaceX from government contract welfare. they are now too embedded, much like Microsoft with their shitty products, and will always recieve enough taxpayer gibs to keep them profitable while also paying investors. literally nobody else can compete

62362863
>>62361452
It's a mystery

62362883
>>62361478
He makes promises he can't keep and retards like you fall for it every time which keep the stocks inflated.

62362985
>>62361452
It's paper networth. His net worth is in stock shares. The stock market has nothing to do with real life and is just a popularity contest. Buying a share of Tesla and thinking you own a piece of Tesla despite it never paying a dividend or doing a buyback is no different than buying dodgecoin and thinking u own a stake in the dog. His stocks only value is just what ever u can tell to for to the next person. All he does is pull from science fiction and that keeps his stock popular. It's literally no different from a casino. Ultimately this gambling money comes from the petro dollar

62363104
>>62362557
Poorfag doesn't bussines

62363313
>>62361452
People are so impressed by the self-landing rockets and the sci fi visions that they throw their money at him without any thought on whether the numbers are reasonable.

62363342
>>62362818
US government contracts are only 21% of SpaceX revenue ("Customer A" in the prospectus), and the growth potential is limited.

There is no strong vendor lock-in with SpaceX like there is with Microsoft. The US government absolutely wants to diversify its vendor base and will give contracts to other vendors at earliest opportunity

62363359
>>62361966
she also has an unlimited customer base

62363375
>>62361478
>EVs and robots
requires the us government to ban chinese evs and robots because they're raping him in price lmao, if elon is worth 1 trillion cuz muh robots then unitree is worth 100 trillion lmao

62363382
>>62361478
He's not ahead of the competition in anything except space.

For a time, he was ahead in EVs. This he achieved by leveraging advancements in battery technology by Japanese and Korean companies, while other car companies were hesitant because they did not want to cannibalize their existing ICE product lines.

62363405
>>62361452
everything is fake and gay in this economy and World retard
>SpaceX's valuation is expected to be nearly $1.8 trillion, revenue was 18 billion in 2025
>Toyota valuation is at 230 billion, revenue in 2025 was 320 billion

62363434
>>62363405
we don't need more cars
we need more spaceships
simple as

62363442
>>62361499
I'll believe sodium batteries when i see them. The voltage curve of the ones i've seen so far are horrible--a car at 100% charge might be fast but at 50% charge might be dog slow.

62363501
>>62363442
No no everything that comes out of China is bleeding edge and perfect on the first try.

62363544
>>62361452
because you keep giving him money

62363603
>>62363375
>Trusting chink shit
Not in my lifetime xiao

62363614
>>62361452
>all ''muh equity' meme, actually has nothing
Nearly all of the modern, popular billionaire class isn't even rich. Augustus or Mansa Musa mog.

62363633
>>62363442
They are a real thing. But they are not premium batteries for cars because of their rather low energy density. Much better suited for energy storage where you don't care about space or weigh that much if the specs are otherwise good.

62363679
>>62362689
>Like many of us, I don't like his liberalism or his transhumanism
...what? When people talk about musk being liberal or a transhumanist isn't the first thing that comes to mind

62363689
>>62363679
Maybe it should be

62364188
>>62362689
i wish neuralink were public id invest the shit out of that high key

62364215
>>62362689
After Elon replied "you have said the whole truth" to a post on twatter that was basically "jews are racist towards white people" it was like a switch was flipped. They drug him to israel to make him look at a babby crib from the mossad oct 7 false flag attack and see their gay wall. The US media jews went full Elon bad and the jews sent their deranged libtards golems after Teslas in the streets. It was a sight be behold. The Elon plate carrier is up there with the Jeb Bush hoodie moment.

62364243
>>62361452
Because him are genius businessman.

62364247
>>62361452
Idk but what he did with twitter is up there with the all time great American shit.

62364330
>>62361452
Hypeman incarnated.

item.title: I got a 20% raise yesterday. $120 --> $144k (plus $7k-$10k annual bonuses). I also just paid off my car which frees up $… — 32 replies — 46.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I got a 20% raise yesterday. $120 --> $144k (plus $7k-$10k annual bonuses). I also just paid off my car which frees up $…

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-13 22:15 UTC

OP

I got a 20% raise yesterday. $120 --> $144k (plus $7k-$10k annual bonuses). I also just paid off my car which frees up $500/month. What should I do with the extra income?

Right now I basically break even when factoring in mortgage, gas/food, insurance, preschool costs, and 5% contribution to retirement (with employer match). I live in a moderately HCOL area, for context. I'm looking at an extra $1800 now per month post-tax. I'm thinking of increasing retirement contributions, put a couple hundred extra a month toward mortgage principal (5.625% interest rate), putting a little into kids college funds, and saving up what remains in HYSA for emergencies and home projects. Anything else I'm missing?

Early replies (first 3)

62363658
not your personal blog, faggot

62363663
go back you wagie normie niggerfaggot

62363668
Grats. Pay min on mortgage, no extra. Put extra into roth ira until maxed. Rest into roth 401k if available.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62363673
You need to max your retirement BEFORE accounting for housing. 401k, roth, and 1k into the market in a brokerage monthly. If this is not possible move to a location that makes this possible

62363674
>>62363668
Why not extra into mortgage? Because interest is low enough that investments will yield more? I just hate being in debt.

62363680
>>62363673
Same question (>>62363674). I can't exactly move. I have one of those hard-to-get, amazing jobs and I can't just get another so easily.

62363696
>>62363674
Im assuming that based off your income and preschool comment you are married with atleast 1 kid. That puts you in basically zero percent tax bracket after 401k match and hsa unlessyour wife also works. Thats when you roth max...or i you have balls margin max in a brokerage, but macro wise now is not the time to do that+roth gives you the option to pull out contributions if needed. Not enough info given to make a call on a lot of advice, but paying down <7% mortgage isnt wise in any case. Maybe if youre close to getting out of pmi range or dont want to insure the house because you can fix it yourself cheaper tjan insurance costs over time.

62363716
>>62363696
Nope I have no PMI. But I have two kids. Mortgage is $3184/month. My wife doesn't work. So my absolute first move should be increase 401k payments, it seems. I can't put all the extra money in it cause I have more short-term goals. Under my new salary, my 5% contribution would be $600/month, matched another $600 by employer. That's $14,420/year. Maybe I'll contribute an extra $465/month to make it an even $20/year. My wife and I have approx. $130k in retirement already (I'm 32, she's 30).

She will hopefully be returning to work in a couple years which will help. Anyways I apologize for the blogposting.

62363745
>>62363674
Yes because you can make much more especially with compounding than that interest rate. Inflation is at least 4% realistically, you can eventually pay that off easier as cash becomes more worthless.
>>62363696
Must be underage. He is no where near 0% bracket at that income. PMI has nothing to do with insuring your house. If you have a mortgage you have to have home insurance. PMI is if you have less than 20% equity in your home it is extra completely separate from home insurance due to being higher risk brokie.

62363770
>>62363716
>wife doesnt work
>2 kids
>preschool
??? Is your wife a retard or a frail Victorian child?

62363775
>>62363716
Just educate yourself on all the tax code and pitfalls and even think outside the box. If your 401k has good fund option, low fees, or is even self directed if youre lucky, I woudn't hesitate to up contributions. I personally max my 401k and have been lucky taking out loans when the market peaks then reinvest in my brokerage after a dip (got one right before the yen carry dump and tariff bullshit). I can grab the tax deduction and still keep the brokerage up, but I wont pretend it wasnt a little luck. If youre only in the 10/12% bracket thats not as much bang for the risk. My job is also pretty safe..biggest risk is me rag quitting and having those loan outstanding is keeping me in a few more years for better or worse.

62363785
>>62363745
Im not even suggesting to pay it down early. Mayb make a few payments if he ws close to 20% or pay it off completely if he wants to forgo insurance entirely or there are declvelopment plans the bank wont like. And if you think someone cant get their taxes down to zero at that income or significiantly higher, youre the brokie.

62363815
>>62363770
>just send $5k+/month to full-time daycare
The preschool is part-time for my older son's benefit; not a childcare center.

62364055
>>62363657
>5% contribution to retirement
that's pretty low (even with employer match), but I wouldn't put any more into the 401k. Plunk the rest of your free cash into a Roth IRA at your favorite brokerage firm (I like Schwab because it's also a FDIC bank, but Vanguard and Fidelity are also fine).

62364061
>>62363657
>Right now I basically break even
i hope your bitch is working.

62364072
>>62363745
>you can make much more especially with compounding
1965-1982 and 2000-2012 say "hi". Paying off the mortgage is a guaranteed win. Playing the market is only a win as long as line goes up.

62364118
Roth IRA 50/50 SCHD and VT

62364143
>>62363657
If you don't have an emergency fund, prioritize that. 3-6 months, but I'd aim closer to 6 with kids. Also, what a lot of people don't consider is building additional dedicated savings funds for known irregular expenses. (For example, you know you will eventually need a new roof, so start putting a little away every month now instead of needing to take out a loan in the future) You could save for a new car, computer, mattress, air conditioning unit, etc. this way. Think of expensive things you own that will eventually need to be replaced and start saving a little each month now for them so that down the line you aren't having to pay some interest to some jew to borrow his money in a lump sum.

The rest of your plan sounds good. Congrats on the raise!

62364145
>>62363674
Can you recast your mortgage? Given that your mortgage interest is greater than the risk-free rate, that would be a reasonable move. But otherwise, you should never put more into your mortgage payments than necessary because it affords you no benefit. If nothing else, you could just save the extra money in an emergency fund, to pay the mortgage in a later month if you lose your source of income.

62364224
>>62364061
The cost of daycare for two kids would basically be $60k/year where we live so there's no point in her working. We were also moving around a lot the last four years to get to my current long-term career so she didn't exactly have the opportunity to career build. She will be working once the younger one is in kindergarten.
>>62364055
Is the idea that it's good to diversify between pre-tax and post-tax contributions?
>>62364143
Yea , you get it. It's why I can't fully commit to retirement funds. We need a new driveway in the next couple years, for instance. This raise makes it possible. The bugger question is what's the ideal breakdown for allocation.
>>62364145
We actually financed from 6.99% to 5.625% back in...April I think? With no points. Literally the day before Trump starting bombing Iran. Mortgage payment was supposed to go down $300/month but property taxes were raised so it only went down $200...

62364230
>>62363657
But do you have seven figures? If not, you're still lower class according to /biz/

62364245
>>62364224
>it's good to diversify between pre-tax and post-tax contributions
Yes. Withdrawing from pre-tax accounts counts as income, and you pay income tax on it. But that income also affects things like medicare payments and so forth, so you want the ability to control how much of your retirement money is income and how much is tax free (Roth). It's also good to diversify between retirement and non-retirement accounts, so set up a regular brokerage account and contribute to it as well, you'll pay capital gains taxes but those are much lower than income taxes (0% up to $98k/yr if you're married filing jointly), and that also doesn't count as income. That'll give you something you can tap into if you or your wife need to retire early, e.g. to take care of a parent or something.

62364248
>>62364224
man you have two kids while being a wagie and having a mortgage?

no one in your entire family tree could give you enough money to be free?

and you think you were good enough to reproduce?

62364257
>>62364224
>what's the ideal breakdown for allocation.
There is no universal ideal, that's something that is specific to your situation. I've got about 60% of my money in pre-tax retirement accounts and I wish it were much lower. Unfortunately I've been ineligible to contribute to a Roth for most of my career, so the other 40% is in a couple of brokerage accounts that I'll have to pay capital gains taxes to access. If I were to do it again I'd only contribute enough to my 401k to get the employer match, then put the rest into an S&P 500 index fund in a brokerage account. If you can contribute to a Roth, then I'd split my non-401k contributions evenly between the Roth and brokerage funds. The 0% tax on Roth accounts is no joke, but not being able to tap into them until you're 59 1/5 is also no joke, meanwhile the relatively low capital gains tax rate takes a lot of the sting out of the brokerage accounts.

62364265
>>62364248
lol
spoilt brat faggot
you'd be penniless if you weren't carried by your parent's money

62364271
>>62364265
>family is on this planet since 2500 B.C
>still does not own a home

Yikes

62364274
>>62364245
And for the regular brokerage account, I take I should tie the money to something like S&P? The only reason I've shied away from that is in my college years I was one if those shitheads addicted to "active investment" and fear I'll blow thousands of dollars again if I open a new account.
>>62364248
>wage
I make $144k/year and work essentially my own hours with no boss. I have to be at work approx. 5 hours/week in the fall and spring (I'll let you guess my job) but that's it. So technically I'm a wagie but I never feel like it. Never have to ask time off for anything.
>>62364257
What happens if I tap before 59.5? Percent penalty? I used to have money in i-series bonds and it sucked not bring able to touch them for however long the maturity date was.

62364288
>>62364271
you have no valuable advice to offer anyone. you live in a sheltered bubble

62364289
>>62364271
OP here, yea, my family is full of financially retarded people. My dad proudly jokes that his Native American name is "Spends With Fury." And because my parents being broke led to me getting a full tuition "scholarship" to college, he feels totally justified in his actions. He tells me he plans to spend every cent of his money before he dies.

I'm doing my best to set my kids up better but inflation and rising housing costs didn't help. I have a buddy whose grandparents and parents set him up nicely. He bought a $3 million home in DC recently, all cash, is spending $750k on renovations, and doesn't even work. Must be nice. I've made my own life nearly as nice as circumstances could have allowed.

62364313
>>62364224
Here's what I would do:
>Emergency fund first, get it to 3 months asap since you have other big known expenses coming up. After that, maybe throw $100 or $200 a month into it to keep it growing while you're doing other things with your money. I really think with kids the ultimate goal should be 6 months.
>You're supposed to have roughly 1x your salary saved for retirement by 30, which you do. What I would do is increase your 401k contributions by 1% annually to make sure you keep on track until you get to about 10%. So bump it up to 6% after this raise. Open a roth IRA if you don't have an IRA already and max that out. Max out an HSA if you have one.
>Put a couple hundred into your kids' college funds
>Look at prices for new driveways in your area and start putting away monthly to pay for it. You could probably push the replacement out a few years if you need to. I mean, it's just a bunch of concrete you drive on top of, it's doesn't have to be pristine. It's not an emergency if it's got a bunch of cracks in it.
>Then increase mortgage payments

Also, look for ways to save money. $144k/year after taxes should leave you about $100k. Subtracting mortgage and the $1800/month you have freed up means you're spending roughly $3300/month on non-mortgage expenses. I'm not saying you need to eat beans for every meal or skip vacations or stop enjoying your hobbies, but take a look at the stuff you buy and think about if you really need to be spending money on that. If you do, fine, but it's worth a look to see if you can free up extra money.
You'll probably be getting more pay raises and your wife will start working in the future too so even if you feel stretched thin now, don't stress about it, you're on the right track and things will get easier.

>I should tie the money to something like S&P?
Yeah, that or total stock market, total world stock market. Any broad index fund you like and then don't touch it.

62364320
>>62364315
are you seriously coping about being a trust fund kid so hard you have memes saved to your computer to use when people point this out online

item.title: Law of the jungle — 68 replies — 28.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 68 • Updated: 2026-06-13 20:32 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387

>/biz/

62361065
>>62360991
literally just pay him to chill. its that easy

62361074
>>62359496

62361079
>>62360200
>kekaroo
This gay aussie /pol/ faggot spams /biz/

62361121
get a trillion dollars and find out what happens
oh, nothing happens? ok

62361214
I know a god will hunting troll when I see one.

62361399
>>62359496
These are BB guns, brownoid.
Google what that is, since you don't know.

62361400
>>62359336
Based

62361423
>>62361048
2 mil in stocks/crypto not 2 mil net worth and unlike you I don't need to cry and piss my pants over rich people

62361436
kinda rude

62361646
>>62361423
plywood box equity doesn't count. and you aren't rich, because you work for a living. literally no one in the history of mankind has created generational worth from working. you need to own a business, which you don't.

62361649
>>62361646
if he has that much in stocks and crypto he probably owns a business.

all you have to do is buy and sell in a single fiscal year and you're technically in business.

62361653
>>62359336
Nigger, you will never do shit, you never have done shit. Your impotent crying school shooter fantasy will not improve you situation, while your mother is getting rutted by the tenth nigger of the day

62361673
>>62360694
kek poorfag and a stinkoid brownoid baggie. i am so much better than you its unreal

62361674
>>62361653
>nigger cuck bbc fantasy posting while defending trillionaire jews online
i am whiter than you

62361730
>>62360200
>>62361079
Interesting fact about "Kekaroo":

Not once in the history of the word "Kekaroo" did the person typing it actually laughed. It's the ultimate "I'm not mad, I'm laughing" internet argument seethecope word.

62361735
>>62361674
>i am whiter than you
>things white people don't say
there's no "whiter"
you either are white or you are not.

you are not

62361872
>>62359336
if you were rich you wouldn't live around the jungle.

62361876
>how do you respond without sounding mad?
BANG BANG BANG BANG

62361887
>>62359400

62362019
>>62359400

62362087
>>62361730

62362109

62362126
>>62359336
I wish stupid people would just stick to being beasts of burden and let the smart people do the things that make society go. He doesnt have a fucking scrooge mcduck money bank brainlet, its company valuation. paper value.

62362156
>>62359336

62362331
>>62359336

>>62359336

will you suck my dick?
will you eat my shit?

62362874

item.title: The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. — 50 replies — 25.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants.

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-13 21:00 UTC

OP

The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. He's upper class. And has 3 passports and owns properties and businesses (wealth) around the world. He isn't waiting on a paycheck to drive down to Costco.
Hint: it's a Rothschild property.
Making even a million bucks a year writing code for Elon or Bezos or Brin does NOT put you in the upper class. At all.
I am European and only we have class. We invented class.

Early replies (first 3)

62356062
>>62356048
Before AI, you could write code for ten years and then buy this.

62356086
Got so mad in the other thread you thought you would start a new thread about it LMAO

62356191
this is that same polish guy who was seething in the other thread about class or whatever

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359194
>>62358695
>Jesus Christ
>demonic kike

62359210
>>62358989
It still is, but you can live your life like a beta peasant trying to extend your life seeking a philosopher stone (if only to perpetuate your suffering) or live like a chad pharaoh accepting death and building his own sarcophagus and tomb during his lifetime. Two different games, yet the same ending

62359212
>>62358695
Where the fuck is Christ? Why has he foresaken me? Is he even fucking real?

62359254
>>62358989
You don't even know the name of the guy who built those.

62359275
>>62356048
yeah and he is never happy and is made of the same carbon we all are. class is fake. wealth is fake. you can be happy with nothing easier than being happy with everything.

62359283
>>62359212
the real god made himself known to man a lot longer than 2000 years ago. it was pre history, so of course we know nothing about it. abrahamic tradition is nonsense.

62359313
>>62356048
Damn I triggered this barely literate 2nd generation immigrant to America (note I never called him an American - he isn't) so bad. Damn, I am almost sorry since it's Friday but not sorry since you're brown.

62359371
>>62356048
I'm sure his life sucks everyone has a reason to cry about shit no amount of stucco will save your body from pain suffering and misery it is inescapable

62359452
>>62358695
God bless you please pray for me.

62359520
>class is following as many rules as possible while frowning and spending lots of money
Wow sounds like a good time sign me up

62359522
>>62358695
Oh please spare me the weak cope philosophy where Jesus christ literally gets abandoned by God when he needs him the most on the cross so there is no guarantee. God is a child's fantasy of cohesion that the world is simple and suffering has meaning, you think someone is watching you at all times and ur not smart enough to realize that emptiness is structural and is born out of language and it's a insurmountable gap that you can only move through and not ever reach. When u move towards it without expecting anything..that is when u can finally act finally be free from god at least,.to relate to the symbolic world of language differently in a way where u can actually do what you want.

62359740
>>62359522
You’re a fag or a drug or porn addict. Maybe all three. Fuck off retard.

62360058
>>62359212
>>62359283
"The fool says in his heart that there is no God"

Maybe try turning away your sinful ways, repenting and searching scripture instead of looking for handouts. Or you could just live out your days as a hard hearted rancid transgressor and go to the grave an unbelieving fool. Nothing anyone says can change your blackened heart. The Holy Spirit does that work.

62360102
>>62360058
why is a 24 year old mulatto born again christian zoomer trying to give me life advice?

62360116
>>62359522
you have free will to do what you want. But be warned that you lash out at the unblemished Lamb of God. The Alpha and the Omega who healed every infirmity and cast out demons from the land. The Good Shepherd who has defeated and and is Seated at the right hand of Power. The sad thing about people like you is that you are so fallen and your nature is so corrupt that you cannot even hear the Truth if it was read to you. Even the demons themselves that Jesus drove out of people KNEW that he was the Son of the Most High. Repent brother. The Kingdom of Heaven is at hand. We know not the day nor the hour of His return

62360814
>>62359005
>these death monuments will be renouned and respected and not some dinky little tourist hotsp-ACK

62361163
>>62357608
Why do people hate on these posts instead of try to figure out ways to improve their quality of life to have similar?

That's the whole point of this board. Instead of hating on people showing off their wealth figure out how to get it too.

I thought it was only like this on hip hop forums. Everyone hat s on each other and then refuse all ways of getting to it.

62361183
>>62359210
Or stop all or nothing thinking and find a way to live well so you can have wealth and not have to compromise yourself in any way?

62362527
>>62356048
Why would I want such a monstrosity.

62362541
>>62360116
>unfathomably based life speaking minister of /biz
I will miss you when the tranny kike mods ban you for speaking truth, but I know you will bear your cross with dignity.

62362654
>>62356048

62362675
>>62362527
You’re not European, you would not get it.

62362885
>>62358695

62363916
>>62356048
everyone there is a pedophile and you'd be asked to leave if you said nigger or faggot. pass.

62363922
>>62356062

62364033
>>62362527

62364139

62364148
